citation: Li, J. and Lee, P. C. 2021. Syntactic Distribution of the Semantic Classes of Dative Verbs in English and Cantonese.

citation: Xu, D. and Peyraube, A. 1997. On the Double Object Construction and the Oblique Construction in Cantonese.

citation: Cheung, L. Y. L. 2007. The Cantonese Dative Constructions: Implications for Processing.
claim_scope: Canonical lexical-GIVE order and elicited heavy-theme alternatives

citation: Wong, A. M. Y. 2023/2024. The bei Constructions in Cantonese: A Corpus-Driven Study.

citation: Lam, O. S. C. 2008. Object Functions and the Syntax of Double Object Constructions in Lexical Functional Grammar.
claim_scope: Lexical-GIVE order and judged heavy-theme alternatives

citation: Ngai, S. 2023. Haplology of Cantonese BEI: Evidence in Production.
claim_scope: Four dative construction forms and a production test of double-畀 conditioning

claim_scope: Lexical-GIVE theme-recipient form and V-theme-畀-recipient serial/benefactive form

claim_scope: Lexical GIVE, transfer versus beneficiary, causative/permissive versus passive ambiguity, and passive role mapping

citation: Chan, K. L. 2021. Bei2 as a Causative and Passive Marker in Cantonese.

citation: Wong, A. M. Y., Chow, D. C. W., McBride-Cheng, C., and Stokes, S. F. 2010. Optional Elements and Variant Structures in the Productions of bei2 'to Give' Dative Constructions in Cantonese-Speaking Adults and Three-Year-Old Children.
claim_scope: Full/non-full, VRT/VTR, prepositional, and serial variants in parent-child conversation

claim_scope: Canonical passive versus NP1-畀-NP2-V-NP3 indirect passive; retained patient/object; corpus count and pragmatic restriction

citation: Chow, P. L. 2019. Passive and Permissive Constructions in Cantonese: An LFG Account.
claim_scope: Passive/permissive disambiguation, indirect-passive object in situ, and distinct analyses of passive and permissive 畀
important_limit: Abstract-level use only until chapter/example locators are recovered; same author program as Chow 2018

citation: Chow, P. L. 2026. Increasing Translation Accuracy for Grammaticalized bei2 in Cantonese: A Corpus-Linguistic Approach to Machine Translation Disambiguation.
claim_scope: Current machine-translation research on grammaticalized 畀 disambiguation
important_limit: Do not use reported percentages or diagnostics until full text and exact locators are verified

citation: Leung, J. R. 2026. Detecting paths of change in the heritage context: Directional motion event expression in Cantonese spoken in Toronto and Hong Kong. Asia-Pacific Language Variation 11(1-2).
publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; author-uploaded accepted manuscript
claim_scope: Directional morphemes as main verbs or complements; locative objects; source and goal; immediately following motion-cum-purpose phrases; purposive 去/嚟 boundary
important_limit: The study's coding decisions and heritage-comparison sample do not define Canto Span nodes or establish unrestricted productivity

citation: Szeto, P. Y. 2015. The Emergence of Perfective Aspect in Cantonese-English Bilingual Children: Bilingual Development and Language Contact. MPhil thesis, The Chinese University of Hong Kong.
claim_scope: Attested adult/model Cantonese motion predicates with perfective 咗 before an overt goal: 去咗圖書館, 返咗屋企, 到咗學校
important_limit: The thesis studies perfective aspect and bilingual development; these examples attest forms but do not establish a parser ontology or the full motion-verb inventory

citation: Chor, W. O. W. 2018. Directional Particles in Cantonese: Form, Function, and Grammaticalization. Amsterdam/Philadelphia: John Benjamins.
claim_scope: Directional complement ordering; 到 as arrival/goal verb or particle; spatial versus nonspatial goals; lexicalized 返工, 返學, and 返屋企 uses
important_limit: The monograph's diachronic and functional analyses do not prescribe Canto Span's runtime labels or make every directional sequence one construction

citation: Kwan, S. W. M. 2010. The Placement of Locative Prepositional Phrases in Cantonese: Processing and Iconicity. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics 8(2): 163-198.
claim_scope: Dominant preverbal 喺 locative phrase order; event-location versus postverbal result-location contrast; static location and source placement
important_limit: The article analyzes locative PP placement, not all predicates, localizers, presentational codas, or Canto Span's umbrella label

citation: Yip, V. and Matthews, S. 2000. Basic Cantonese: A Grammar and Workbook. London/New York: Routledge.
verified_locator: PDF pp. 77-80, 105-108, and 134-135; Unit 13 printed pp. 65-69, Unit 18 printed pp. 93-96, and Unit 23 printed pp. 122-123
claim_scope: 喺 locatives and localizers; 由 source; 向 direction; 經 route/via; outer aspect 咗/過; V-咗-O and result/current-relevance contrasts; final 未 questions after explicit completion/result particles or aspect markers
important_limit: A teaching reference supplies descriptive examples and contrasts, not corpus frequency, exhaustive diagnostics, universal speaker judgments, or a parser ontology; its completion-question examples do not license arbitrary bare VP + 未.

citation: Leung, H. H. 2014. Cantonese Coverbs: A Syntactic Reanalysis. MA qualifying paper, University of California, Berkeley.
claim_scope: Coverb phrases precede the main verb; inventory includes 喺 location, 向 direction, 由 source, and 經 path/via; category-analysis dispute
important_limit: This unpublished theory paper is corroborative only and cannot by itself promote a parser construction

citation: Matthews, S. 2006. On Serial Verb Constructions in Cantonese. In A. Y. Aikhenvald and R. M. W. Dixon (eds.), Serial Verb Constructions: A Cross-Linguistic Typology, 69-87. Oxford University Press.
claim_scope: Cantonese serial-verb diagnostics; single-clause/single-predicate analysis; subject sharing; purpose-infinitive, compound, complement, and coordination boundaries
important_limit: The chapter explicitly shows that classification may be indeterminate; surface adjacency and subject sharing do not independently prove a purpose relation

citation: Wong, Anita Mei-Yin. 2023. Understanding Development and Disorder in Cantonese Using Language Sample Analysis. Routledge.
publication_type: research monograph using natural language samples
verified_locator: book pp. 44-49 and 95-96; negation, resultative/directional/potential structures, examples 21, 23-27, 37, and 40
claim_scope: Natural language samples and coding distinctions for preverbal negation, resultative/directional/potential structures, compound directionals, 返到學校, 返學, and a spatial localizer complement
important_limit: Clinical/developmental language-sample framework; attestations and coding categories do not prove unrestricted adult productivity, one settled theoretical analysis, or Canto Span node boundaries.

citation: Shan, Yunming and Jin, Lixin. 2025. 粵語位移事件編碼類型再探 [Revisiting the Encoding Typology of Motion Events in Cantonese]. Language and Linguistics 26(2).
publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; corpus-informed motion-event typology
verified_locator: §§3.1-3.3 and 4.1; examples 1-20 and 29-35; published online 17 March 2025
claim_scope: Independent path verbs; direction-plus-deixis sequences; manner-plus-path sequences; three-way contrast among single-verb, serial/path-verb, and resultative motion encoding; aspect-position diagnostics
important_limit: The article advances a serial-verb/path-verb analysis that competes with complement analyses; its typology does not validate Canto Span labels or license every lexical combination.

citation: Yiu, Carine Yuk-man (姚玉敏). 2016. 粵語繼續體「落去」和開始體「起嚟」的產生. In 漢語研究的新貌：方言、語法與文獻 / New Horizons in the Study of Chinese: Dialectology, Grammar, and Philology, pp. 261-284.
publication_type: research chapter in an academic edited volume
claim_scope: Directional lexemes as predicates or complements; come/go deixis; manner-plus-direction; complex directionals; object-order differences; potential V-得/唔-directional restrictions; directional-to-aspect grammaticalization
important_limit: The chapter focuses on 落去 and 起嚟 and documents lexical asymmetries; it does not establish a uniform productive class or one uncontested syntactic ontology.

citation: Liang, Yuqing and Mai, Ziyin. 2025. Supplementary materials for Grammatical development of the native L1 in Cantonese-English bilingual children: early costs and long-term gains. Bilingualism: Language and Cognition.
publication_type: supplementary coding scheme for a peer-reviewed empirical article
verified_locator: Appendix S1, Table S1, structures 11, 15, and 16; PDF pp. 4-5
claim_scope: Operational separation of preverbal negation adverbs, simple/compound/tri-syllabic directional particles, and postverbal potential patterns
important_limit: The coding scheme is adapted from Wong 2023 and is corroborative operational evidence, not an independent theory of directional syntax or productivity.

citation: Cheng, Lisa Lai-Shen and Rint Sybesma. 1999. Bare and Not-So-Bare Nouns and the Structure of NP. Linguistic Inquiry 30(4): 509-542.
claim_scope: Distribution, interpretation, and constituent structure of Cantonese bare nouns and classifier+noun phrases.
important_limit: The article advances a particular syntactic analysis and predates later evidence of geographic and speaker variation; it does not prescribe Canto Span node names or object-role labels.

citation: Simpson, Andrew, Hooi Ling Soh, and Hiroki Nomoto. 2011. Bare classifiers and definiteness: A cross-linguistic investigation. Studies in Language 35(1): 168-193.
verified_locator: pp. 168-172; examples 1-3 and discussion of Hong Kong and Guangzhou Cantonese variation
claim_scope: Definition and interpretation of bare-classifier forms, including Cantonese classifier+noun expressions in subject and object positions.
important_limit: Elicitation results establish variation in definite-reference preferences; they do not license one invariant definiteness rule or a classifier-alone nominal.

citation: Yu, Dominic. 2006. Relative clauses and nominal modifiers in Cantonese. Unpublished research paper, University of California, Berkeley.
claim_scope: Cantonese nominal order, possessive classifier phrases, demonstrative-classifier phrases, and headless demonstrative+classifier NPs.
important_limit: Not peer reviewed and partly theoretical; use only as corroboration or narrow evidence for explicitly illustrated forms, never as sole authority for productivity.

citation: Bond, Francis and Joanna Ut-Seong Sio. 2024. A Construction-based Approach to Cantonese Classifiers. Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Head-Driven Phrase Structure Grammar, 60-75.
publication_type: peer-reviewed conference paper with implemented grammar
claim_scope: Four Cantonese NP schemata D-(X)-C-N, X-C-N, C-N, and N; classifier cardinality and cognitive-status distinctions; implemented HPSG analysis.
important_limit: The HPSG analysis is one explicit formalization, not a settled universal ontology; its semantic type hierarchy should not be imported wholesale into Canto Span.

citation: Lam, Charles, Chaak Ming Lau, and Jackson L. Lee. 2024. Multi-Tiered Cantonese Word Segmentation. Proceedings of LREC-COLING 2024, 11993-12002.
publication_type: peer-reviewed conference paper and corpus annotation scheme
claim_scope: Overt segmentation and surface composition of numeral-classifier, possessor-classifier, determiner-classifier, and noun-ellipsis expressions in Cantonese.
important_limit: The paper primarily specifies word segmentation rather than a complete syntax or semantics; examples corroborate surface form and overt boundaries only.

citation: Xia, Hengliang. 2025. Syntax of Classifiers and Measure Words in Three Chinese Languages. Proceedings of the 2025 Annual Conference of the Canadian Linguistic Association.
claim_scope: Cantonese Num-CL-N, Dem-CL-N, bare CL-N, Poss-CL-N distribution; coordination and noun-ellipsis diagnostics; limits on inserting jat1.
important_limit: Comparative judgments and the proposed right-branching analysis are author analyses; use distributional contrasts as evidence but retain competing structural analyses.

citation: Sio, Joanna Ut-Seong and Francis Bond. 2025. Inner and outer aspect in Cantonese. HPSG 2025 conference paper.
publication_type: peer-reviewed conference paper / published conference PDF
claim_scope: Outer aspect inventory including perfective 咗; distinction between event-linked and individual-predicative inner material; fixed V-IAP-OAP ordering; 完 as endpoint-adding inner particle; 我食完(咗)麵
important_limit: The paper explicitly implements only an illustrative subset; its HPSG representation and selected co-occurrence table do not establish an exhaustive Cantonese inventory, unrestricted lexical productivity, or Canto Span node boundaries.

citation: Sybesma, Rint. 2013. Cantonese sin1 先 and the question of microvariation and macrovariation. In Guangshun Cao, Hilary Chappell, Redouane Djamouri, and Thekla Wiebusch (eds.), Breaking Down the Barriers: Interdisciplinary Studies in Chinese Linguistics and Beyond, 971-994. Taipei: Academia Sinica.
publication_type: peer-reviewed academic book chapter; author-uploaded full text
claim_scope: Published example 佢食完咗啲飯 and discussion in which a verbal particle precedes perfective 咗 before the overt object
important_limit: The chapter is centrally about postverbal 先, not a comprehensive aspect grammar; one author example supports the surface order but not universal speaker preference or the full CompletionVP/PerfectiveVP inventory.

citation: Fan, Xiaolei and Kin Wing Kevin Chan. 2022. 香港粵語「咗」的語法特點：與北京話「了1」的比較 [Grammatical properties of zo 咗 in Hong Kong Cantonese: A comparative study with le1 了1 in Beijing Mandarin]. Language and Linguistics 23(3): 371-410.

important_limit: The article supports form and placement claims, not Canto Span labels or unrestricted completion-marker substitution; crawler-visible text was used for the exact example rather than a locally archived PDF.

citation: Law, Ann. 2001. A-not-A Questions in Cantonese. UCL Working Papers in Linguistics 13: 295-318.
publication_type: peer-reviewed/edited university working-paper article; author full text
claim_scope: Cantonese A-not-A syntax; eligible predicate/modal categories; co-occurrence restrictions; modal subtypes; copular 係唔係 analysis; neutral-context contrast.
important_limit: Minimalist analysis and author judgments are theory-dependent; some generalizations conflict with other descriptions or samples and do not prescribe Canto Span node boundaries.

citation: Li, Clare. 2017. The Syntactic and Pragmatic Properties of A-not-A Question in Chinese. MA thesis, University of Canterbury.
publication_type: university MA thesis with full repository text
verified_locator: PDF pp. 7, 12-15, 49-55, 74-87; especially examples (1), (7)-(11) and conclusion examples (1)-(4)
claim_scope: Attested Cantonese A-not-A surfaces from three Hong Kong films; 唔 negator; monosyllabic and reduced disyllabic patterns; modal/adjectival forms; final particles; pragmatic uses.
important_limit: Small film/dubbing/subtitle sample and thesis analysis are not an exhaustive grammar or frequency model; absence from the sample is not evidence of impossibility.

citation: Hara, Yurie. 2023. Cantonese Question Particles. In Discourse Particles in Asian Languages, Volume I: East Asia. Routledge.
publication_type: peer-reviewed academic book chapter; author full-text manuscript and publisher metadata
verified_locator: author PDF pp. 1-2 and 13-17; example (1a); Figure 2; conclusion
claim_scope: Contrast among Cantonese polar-question types; 有冇 + VP A-not-A example; expectation/bias analysis; forced-choice and naturalness-rating experiments.
important_limit: The semantic analysis and limited experimental contexts do not establish a hard parser licensing condition; the author explicitly notes private-belief/context complications.

citation: Yip, Moira. 1988. Negation in Cantonese as a Lexical Rule. Bulletin of the Institute of History and Philology 59: 449-477.
publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; official archive full text
verified_locator: pp. 449-477; abstract and opening discussion of 唔, suppletive 有/冇, eligible lexical categories, and A-not-A reduplication
claim_scope: Lexical-rule analysis of Cantonese negation and A-not-A formation; special suppletive negative of 有; broad lexical category eligibility.
important_limit: This is a theory-specific lexical analysis drawing heavily on Yau 1973; use it as a competing formal account and source of distributional observations, not as the sole parser architecture.

citation: Fan, Xiaolei. 2026. The Modal Meaning of the jau5 (有) have+VP in Hong Kong Cantonese. Journal of Chinese Linguistics 54(2): 458-479.
publication_type: peer-reviewed journal article; official journal abstract and author publication metadata
verified_locator: official abstract; Journal of Chinese Linguistics 54(2):458-479; Peking University author publication list
claim_scope: Auxiliary/modal 有 + dynamic VP in Hong Kong Cantonese and its preferential occurrence in polar questions, conditionals, and negatives.
